name: User Access Review
description: User access to systems and data is periodically reviewed to ensure access remains appropriate and aligned with each individual's role. User access reviews reduce the risk of unauthorised or excessive access by confirming that only authorised individuals retain access and that access is revoked when no longer required.

attributes.text: Access reviews are performed on a periodic basis (e.g. quarterly)
testable_criteria: Evidence shows an access review was performed for the period under audit
The review cadence is defined (e.g. quarterly) and documented
The review was performed within the defined cadence (no missed or overdue review periods)
The review has a clear start and completion date recorded

attributes.text: Access is reviewed and approved by an appropriate system or data owner
testable_criteria: The reviewer/approver is identified by name for each user access reviewed
The reviewer is the designated system or data owner (or delegate) for the in-scope system
Evidence of formal approval (sign-off, ticket, signature, or equivalent) is retained
The reviewer is independent of the user being reviewed (a user did not approve their own access)
The review covers all users with access to the in-scope system for the period

attributes.text: Inappropriate or excessive access identified during the review is remediated in a timely manner
testable_criteria: All access flagged as inappropriate or excessive during the review is identified in the review output
A remediation action (revocation or adjustment) is recorded for each flagged item
Evidence confirms the remediation was actually performed in the system (not just requested)
Remediation was completed in a timely manner relative to the review completion date
No flagged items remain open or unresolved without documented justification
